creator: bahlgren@beah.se
text:
Hiroki Sato <hrs@FreeBSD.org> writes:

> Bengt Ahlgren <bengta@sics.se> wrote
>   in <uh7hc4xpi81.fsf@P142.sics.se>:
>
> be> Hiroki Sato <hrs@FreeBSD.org> writes:
> be>
> be> >  So, can you try to create a simple wrapper script that removes
> be> >  LD_LIBRARY_PATH and exec kprinter, and then enter its filename in the
> be> >  print dialog?
> be>
> be> Yes, I have tried that - it works!  (The simpler "env -u
> be> LD_LIBRARY_PATH kprinter" directly in the print dialogue works too.)
>
>  Okay, thanks.  I am now considering to add something that make the
>  trick enabled by default.

Great!  Beware, however, that it also launches the help viewer program
which does need the LD_LIBRARY_PATH.  Otherwise my patch to the
acroread launch script worked nicely (see previous posts).

(The help viewer is however broken in another way, so perhaps it does
not matter... another PR about that in a minute!)

Bengt
